title: SLO/SLI Observability Framework
summary: Built reliability frameworks with SLOs, error budgets, and real-time dashboards for AI and enterprise services.

content.value: Without formal SLOs, incidents were reactive and ad-hoc. Teams had no shared definition of reliability, making it hard to prioritize toil vs. feature work or justify reliability investments.

Defined SLIs for availability, latency, and error rate across all critical services. Translated these into SLOs with rolling 30-day error budget tracking. Built Grafana dashboards surfacing live error budget burn rates, alerting thresholds, and service health at a glance.

Implemented multi-window, multi-burn-rate alerting in Prometheus to catch slow burns before they exhausted error budgets. PagerDuty routing policies were tied directly to error budget consumption rate rather than raw metric thresholds.

Outcome: achieved 99.99% uptime across core payment services, reduced mean time to recovery (MTTR) from 45 minutes to under 15 minutes, and gave product teams data to make informed reliability trade-offs.
